rationale: The citing paper’s core model explicitly includes “influencer effects” and social-network peer influence in diffusion, while the cited paper provides causal evidence on asymmetric opinion-leader peer effects and marketing spillovers in a related marketing-social-interactions domain. Given this strong construct overlap (opinion leaders/peer influence) and close field proximity (both JMR, adjacent years), this does not look like an obvious incidental mention. Even without in-text citation context, it is plausible the cited work serves as a substantive theory/mechanism benchmark source.
